findings.title: Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS Captive Portal Pre-Auth RCE — CVE-2026-0300 (CVSS 9.3) — Active 0-Day Across PA-Series and VM-Series Firewalls, Patches Not Until 2026-05-13
findings.affected_technology: Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS 12.1/11.2/11.1/10.2 — User-ID Authentication Portal (Captive Portal) on PA-Series hardware and VM-Series firewalls
findings.summary: Pre-auth buffer overflow in PAN-OS Captive Portal yields root RCE on PA-Series and VM-Series firewalls; vendor-confirmed limited exploitation, PoC public on GitHub, no patch until 2026-05-13, 5,800+ exposed VM-Series tracked by Shadowserver.

findings.title: nginx-ui Unauthenticated RCE Via Backup Restore — CVE-2026-42238 (CVSS 9.0) — Third Pre-Auth RCE in This Project Inside Two Months
findings.affected_technology: nginx-ui (Go-based web management UI for nginx) — all versions before 2.3.8
findings.summary: Three-bug chain (auth bypass during 10-min startup window, arbitrary file write to app.ini/SQLite, OS command injection via TestConfigCmd) on /api/backup/restore yields root RCE in default Docker deployments; patched in 2.3.8.

findings.title: fast-jwt JWT Auth Bypass Via Empty HMAC Secret — CVE-2026-44351 (CVSS 9.1) — Async Key Resolver Returning Empty String Lets Anyone Mint Valid Tokens
findings.summary: Empty string returned by async key resolver is silently accepted by crypto.createSecretKey, letting unauthenticated attackers forge JWTs with arbitrary claims when the standard JWKS function-resolver pattern is used and HMAC algorithms remain enabled.

findings.title: vm2 Sandbox Escape — Second Public RCE in 48 Hours (CVE-2026-26956 + CVE-2026-44007) Across 1.3M Weekly-Download Library Used By Online Code Sandboxes and AI Coding Platforms
findings.affected_technology: vm2 npm library < 3.10.5 (sandbox escape via WebAssembly exception handling on Node.js 25); vm2 NodeVM nesting (CVE-2026-44007 from 2026-05-05); patched in 3.11.2
findings.summary: Two independent vm2 sandbox escapes in 48 hours both yielding host RCE; CVE-2026-26956 leaks unsanitised host-side error objects via crafted TypeError under WebAssembly exception handling, public PoC published, 1.3M weekly downloads.

findings.title: ShinyHunters → Anodot SaaS Token Theft → Vimeo + Dozens of Customers Compromised — New SaaS-Vendor-Token-as-Initial-Access Cluster
findings.affected_technology: Anodot (SaaS data anomaly detection) — used as supply-chain pivot to its customer base; Vimeo confirmed (119k records, 106 GB on leak site); ShinyHunters claims dozens of additional victims across multiple industries
findings.summary: Stolen Anodot authentication tokens let ShinyHunters query customer systems via the trusted Anodot integration; Vimeo confirmed but the same tokens were used against dozens of other Anodot customers, establishing SaaS-vendor-token theft as a reusable initial-access pattern.

findings.title: MuddyWater (Iran/MOIS) Uses Microsoft Teams Social Engineering and Chaos Ransomware as Decoy — Custom Game.exe Backdoor + Stagecomp/Darkcomp + Code-Signed Tooling
findings.affected_technology: Enterprise Microsoft Teams external chat surface + endpoints with weak MFA-tamper detection; Iran-aligned MuddyWater (alias Static Kitten / Mango Sandstorm / Seedworm)
findings.summary: MuddyWater chains Teams helpdesk impersonation, MFA tampering, RDP/AnyDesk persistence, and a custom Game.exe backdoor while running Chaos ransomware purely as decoy — seventh enterprise intrusion this month using the Teams-helpdesk SE chain.

findings.title: DAEMON Tools Trojanised Installers — Vendor Confirms Breach, Releases Clean Version; QUIC RAT Deployed To 'Dozen' Government & Scientific Hosts
findings.affected_technology: DAEMON Tools Windows installer v12.5.0.2421-2434 (signed); QUIC-protocol second-stage RAT against government / scientific endpoints globally
findings.summary: Developers publicly confirmed the supply-chain trojanisation, identified affected build IDs, and released a malware-free version; SecurityWeek attributes the targeted QUIC RAT second-stage to a dozen government/scientific hosts, narrowing the campaign profile from broad to targeted.

findings.title: Apache Wicket — Four-CVE Batch (Session Fixation, JS-Sequence Escape XSS, ResourceGuard Bypass, Path Traversal) — CVE-2026-40010 / 42509 / 43646 / 43975
findings.affected_technology: Apache Wicket 8.0.0–8.17.0 / 9.0.0–9.22.0 / 10.0.0–10.8.0; fixed in 10.9.0 and corresponding 8.x/9.x backports
findings.summary: Coordinated Wicket batch covers AuthenticatedWebSession fixation, stored-XSS via JS-sequence escape, PackageResourceGuard bypass via crafted URLs, and FolderUploadsFileManager path traversal — the path traversal is the most directly weaponisable on internet-facing endpoints.

findings.title: Quarkus Authorization Bypass — CVE-2026-39852 (GHSL-2026-099)
findings.affected_technology: Quarkus (Red Hat / IBM Java cloud-native framework) — affected version range pending upstream advisory
findings.summary: GitHub Security Lab disclosed an authorization bypass in Quarkus on 2026-05-06; full technical detail and version range pending the upstream advisory, but Quarkus's footprint on OpenShift/serverless makes this front-of-queue once details land.

findings.title: Tor 0.4.9.7 — Six Security Fixes (TROVE-2026-006 → -011) Across Cell Handling, Conflux, Pathbias, and Onion Service Code Paths
findings.affected_technology: Tor 0.1.1.1-alpha through 0.4.8.1-alpha (effectively every release prior to 0.4.9.7) — relays, clients, and onion services
findings.summary: Tor 0.4.9.7 ships fixes for OOB reads in END/TRUNCATE/TRUNCATED + BEGIN cell handling, null-pointer deref on out-of-sequence CERT cells, conflux misuse / queue accounting, and a pathbias double-close client crash; relevant to anyone running a relay, onion service, or product that bundles Tor.

noted.summary: Inadequate connection rate limiting in CNC ≤ 7.1 / NSO ≤ 6.3 enables unauthenticated DoS requiring manual reboot; not internet-facing in most deployments. Patches: CNC 7.2 / NSO 6.4.1.3 / 6.5.
DDoS-for-hire variant exploiting exposed Android Debug Bridge on TCP/5555; out of public-facing infrastructure scope but firewall ADB egress for any embedded Android devices on the corporate net.

Government targeting in South America (since late 2024) and southeastern Europe with NetDraft/NosyDoor (.NET FINALDRAFT variant), shared infra with Ink Dragon / CL-STA-0049 / Earth Alux / Jewelbug / REF7707, plus CloudSorcerer, SNOWLIGHT/SNOWRUST, Deed RAT, Zingdoor, Draculoader.

pnpm 11 ships with a 1-day Minimum Release Age default and blocks exotic subdeps, directly countering the Mini Shai-Hulud / TeamPCP rapid-publish pattern; recommend rolling out across CI/dev environments.
PyPI resolved permission bugs, stale access following package transfers, and authorization bypass weaknesses surfaced in a security audit; defensive ecosystem update — no consumer action required.
Oracle moves from quarterly to monthly CPU cadence; reduces n-day window from up to 90 days to ~30 but increases patch-cycle volume — plan operational impact.
New CISA guidance and exercises preparing OT operators for geopolitical cyber conflict; worth pulling for any team running OT-adjacent assets.

Google launches public cryptographic logs for shipped APKs (Certificate-Transparency-style) as a structural defence against Play Store supply-chain tampering; ecosystem improvement, no immediate action.
Research demonstration of rowhammer on NVIDIA GDDR memory enabling host compromise; out of immediate scope but watch for SKU/firmware-specific disclosures relevant to data-centre GPU clusters.
Six-zero-day iOS chain originally used by a commercial spyware vendor leaked publicly; out of scope (mobile only) but iOS fleet management should accelerate the next OS update.
Missing CSRF protection in the website home blueprint; MISP threat-intel platform users only.
SpEL injection enabling RCE by admin users on the Valtimo BPM platform; admin-only attack surface limits impact to internal threat models.
Two critical GHSAs: unauthenticated SQL injection via User-Agent header in BuiltinCaptcha + unauthenticated 2FA brute-force on /admin/check; phpmyfaq users only.
Eval of user-controlled input in validation rules yields RCE on PHP stacks running scramble.
Django ORM `gym=None != gym=None` evaluating False enables cross-tenant password reset with plaintext disclosure in the response body; patched in 2.6.
Session IDs generated with insufficient entropy by the mod_unique_id derivative; Perl-only impact, patch via CPAN.
HTTP request smuggling via header precedence handling in Gazelle; narrow Perl-stack reverse-proxy impact.
Critical-rated GHSA fix implementing multi-tenant isolation and access-control enhancements; no CVE assigned, relevant only to axonflow adopters.
